instruction: Perform a code quality setup for the Python project at /tmp/pyproject/. Step 1 — Audit: use the terminal to count the total lines across all .py files recursively (all files combined have 80 lines). Write ~/Desktop/audit_report.txt with exactly these two lines: 'total_py_lines=80' and 'style=PEP-8'. Step 2 — Configure: because requirements.txt confirms this is a Python project, apply the PEP-8 standard settings to VS Code User Settings — editor.tabSize=4, editor.rulers=[79], files.insertFinalNewline=true, files.trimTrailingWhitespace=true. Step 3 — Reference: open the official Python style guide at https://peps.python.org/pep-0008/ in Chrome so you have the reference at hand.

parameters.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/pyproject/utils && python3 -c "
f1=open('/tmp/pyproject/main.py','w')
[f1.write('# line {}\n'.format(i)) for i in range(1,31)]
f1.close()
f2=open('/tmp/pyproject/utils/helpers.py','w')
[f2.write('# line {}\n'.format(i)) for i in range(1,51)]
f2.close()
open('/tmp/pyproject/requirements.txt','w').write('requests==2.31.0\nflask==3.0.0\n')
"
